narrative_summary: The **BRAF V600E** variant is a well-characterized oncogenic mutation [PMC12345]. It leads to constitutional activation of the BRAF kinase and the downstream MAPK pathway [PMID: 67890]. This mutation is frequently found in melanoma, colorectal, and thyroid cancers, where it serves as a key diagnostic and therapeutic marker. Functional studies have demonstrated its role in promoting cell proliferation and survival [12345]. While mostly reported as a clear driver mutation, some studies note that its impact can be modulated by co-occurring mutations or the tissue of origin [PMC12345].

article_evidences.overall_article_conclusion: BRAF V600E is a strong driver in melanoma with high therapeutic relevance.

evidence.description: Increased basal kinase activity and downstream phosphorylation of MEK.
evidence.study_context: In vitro kinase assays using purified proteins.
evidence.quoted_text: Basal kinase activity of BRAF V600E was found to be approximately 10-fold higher than that of wild-type BRAF.

evidence.description: Associated with poor prognosis in patients without targeted therapy.
evidence.quoted_text: Patients harboring the V600E mutation showed a significantly shorter overall survival.

article_evidences.uncertainties_or_limitations: Small sample size for the sub-analysis.

article_evidences.overall_article_conclusion: High prevalence of V600E in papillary thyroid carcinoma.

evidence.description: Present in 45% of tested PTC cases, absent in healthy controls.
evidence.quoted_text: The V600E variant was identified in 225/500 PTC cases but 0/1000 healthy controls.

evidence.description: SIFT and PolyPhen-2 scores both indicate high impact.
evidence.study_context: In silico prediction using standard tools.

evidence.description: Alternative splicing might reduce the impact in certain tissues.
evidence.study_context: Transcriptomic analysis of various cell lines.
evidence.quoted_text: In lung tissue, the presence of isoform B may counteract the oncogenic signaling of the V600E mutation.

article_evidences.uncertainties_or_limitations: Predictions not yet validated in vivo for all tissues.
